steps.title: Expand the square
steps.content:
Expand the left side of the given equation.

$$(x+3)^2=x^2+6x+9$$

So the equation becomes

$$x^2+6x+9=28$$

steps.title: Isolate the requested expression
steps.content: The question asks for $x^2+6x$, so subtract $9$ from both sides. $$x^2+6x=28-9$$ $$x^2+6x=19$$ The answer is A.
